Name: dequantization_tensor_optimization
Title: Efficient Dequantization: Leveraging Tensor Cores for Low-Precision Inference
Short Hypothesis: By developing a specialized kernel that combines dequantization and computation into a single tensor core operation, we can significantly reduce the latency and memory overhead typically associated with low-precision inference in large language models.
Related Work: Existing work such as Quantix highlights the inefficiencies of dequantization in quantized models, yet it primarily focuses on memory access patterns rather than optimizing the actual computational workload during dequantization. Our proposal distinctly targets the fusion of dequantization and computation, directly leveraging tensor core capabilities, which has not been thoroughly explored.
Abstract: This proposal aims to enhance the efficiency of low-precision inference in large language models (LLMs) by optimizing the dequantization process. We hypothesize that a specialized kernel can be developed to fuse dequantization with computation, leveraging the capabilities of NVIDIA tensor cores to achieve significant reductions in latency and memory overhead. The proposed approach will involve profiling current dequantization methods, identifying bottlenecks in latency and throughput, and designing a kernel that integrates these operations. We will evaluate this kernel against existing methods, focusing on metrics such as wall-clock latency and tokens-per-second performance on commodity GPUs. This research aims to bridge the gap between low-precision operations and high-performance computing, providing practical solutions that can be readily deployed in real-world scenarios.
Experiments: Profile existing dequantization kernels to establish baseline latency and throughput metrics.
Design and implement a fused dequantization-computation kernel optimized for NVIDIA tensor cores.
Conduct A/B tests comparing the fused kernel against traditional dequantization methods across various LLM architectures.
Measure p50 and p99 latency, throughput under batching, and energy consumption per token to evaluate performance improvements.
Risk Factors and Limitations: Potential risks include the complexity of accurately profiling and optimizing the fused kernel, as well as ensuring compatibility across different model architectures. Limitations may arise from hardware constraints if tensor core capabilities differ across GPU models. Furthermore, while we aim for improvements in throughput and latency, trade-offs in accuracy must also be carefully monitored.
